title: network_segmentation_failure
description: A misconfigured switch VLAN allows a compromised guest Wi-Fi device to reach the internal production network, bypassing all perimeter controls.

timeline.title: Anomalous Internal Access
timeline.description: IDS alerts on port scans originating from the guest Wi-Fi subnet (192.168.100.0/24) reaching production servers on VLAN 10.

timeline.title: VLAN Misconfiguration Found
timeline.description: Network team discovers a trunk port was misconfigured without a native VLAN restriction, allowing guest traffic to reach production VLANs.

timeline.title: Production Systems Probed
timeline.description: The guest device has successfully connected to several internal services including a database server and HR portal using default credentials.

timeline.title: Guest Network Isolated
timeline.description: The misconfigured trunk port is corrected. Guest VLAN traffic is restricted to internet-only access. Internal access blocked.

timeline.title: Compromised Services Assessed
timeline.description: All services accessed by the guest device are audited. Default credentials changed. Access logs reviewed for data exfiltration.

timeline.title: Network Segmentation Audit
timeline.description: Full VLAN and firewall ruleset audit completed. Automated configuration compliance checking implemented.

node-1.title: Guest Device Reaching Production Network
node-1.situation: IDS alerts show port scanning activity from IP 192.168.100.55 (guest Wi-Fi range) against production servers on 10.0.10.0/24. Guest network traffic should be completely isolated from production. A VLAN misconfiguration is suspected.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Block the guest device at the firewall immediately, identify and correct the VLAN misconfiguration on the switch, and alert the IR team.

options.label: B
options.text: Send a warning to the guest user via the Wi-Fi captive portal and monitor for further activity.

options.label: C
options.text: Disable the entire guest Wi-Fi network until the issue is fully understood.

node-2.title: Production Systems Already Accessed
node-2.situation: Investigation reveals the guest device connected to a database server and HR portal using default credentials before the firewall block was applied. You need to determine what data was accessed or exfiltrated.
node-2.question: How do you assess the breach?

options.label: A
options.text: Review access logs on the database and HR portal, identify all queries and downloads performed, and engage the data protection officer to assess regulatory obligations.

options.label: B
options.text: Change the default credentials and assume no sensitive data was accessed since the access was brief.

node-3.title: Fix the VLAN Misconfiguration
node-3.situation: Log analysis shows limited data exposure. The root cause is a misconfigured trunk port that allowed VLAN hopping. You must now remediate the segmentation failure and prevent recurrence.
node-3.question: How do you fix the network segmentation?

options.label: A
options.text: Correct the trunk port configuration with explicit VLAN allowed lists, enable VLAN pruning, and implement ACLs between VLANs at the layer-3 switch or firewall.

options.label: B
options.text: Simply assign the guest network to a different IP subnet without fixing the VLAN trunk configuration.

node-4.title: Validate and Audit All Segmentation
node-4.situation: The specific misconfiguration is fixed. However, this incident reveals that other switch configurations may have similar issues. A comprehensive segmentation audit is needed.
node-4.question: What do you implement for ongoing assurance?

options.label: A
options.text: Conduct a full switch configuration audit against a hardening baseline, implement automated compliance checks for VLAN configurations, and schedule quarterly network segmentation reviews.

options.label: B
options.text: Only audit the switch where the misconfiguration was found and rely on periodic manual reviews.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The network segmentation failure was identified and remediated. VLAN trunk configurations were hardened, automated compliance checks are active, and a quarterly audit schedule ensures ongoing segmentation integrity.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
